draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer interfaces that feel like talking to a helpful colleague rather than reading corporate documentation
facade.label: Chatty and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace warm, approachable microcopy and dialogue-driven interactions throughout the workspace
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into clear, formal, efficiency-focused language and traditional UI patterns without personality

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high-contrast layouts with dark navigation and bright content areas for focus
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light canvas
facade.acceptImplies: Probe contrast ratios, dark UI patterns, and separation between nav and workspace
facade.rejectImplies: Explore softer gradients, monochromatic schemes, or unified light/dark approaches

evidence.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer interfaces that feel like talking to a helpful colleague rather than reading corporate documentation
evidence.implication: Embrace warm, approachable microcopy and dialogue-driven interactions throughout the workspace

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer snappy, zero-latency interactions over smooth transitions that telegraph every state change
facade.axisTargeted: Latency vs. fluidity — whether micro-interactions should feel instantaneous or choreographed
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: Build with snap-back effects, quick state flips, minimal loading states; users want to feel in control and unobstructed
facade.rejectImplies: Favor smooth easing curves, animated state transitions, gentle loading indicators; users want visual reassurance and slower reveal

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent navigation anchor (sidebar) paired with flexible, modular content cards rather than tab-based layouts, because it reduces cognitive load when switching between workspace contexts
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure + content containment (sidebar vs tabs; cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with floating cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore sidebar-first layouts with card-based content systems; test persistent left navigation as primary organizational pattern
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward tab-based or top-navigation models; explore list-based or stream layouts instead of discrete cards

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers warmth and ambient comfort over stark clarity in their AI workspace
facade.label: Soft shadows and glow
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into calming, diffused lighting—curves, rounded corners, gentle color gradients. Make the interface feel less clinical, more inviting.
facade.rejectImplies: User may want crispness and clarity. Push toward high contrast, sharp lines, defined edges, and cooler palettes. The workspace should feel precise and alert.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics only
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy responses in interface behavior

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy responses in interface behavior
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer dense data visualization and metrics displayed as figures rather than narrative explanations
facade.axisTargeted: Information density & format preference (charts/numbers vs text/narrative)
facade.label: Numbers over words
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace design should prioritize quantitative dashboards, metric cards, and data-driven interfaces over prose descriptions and contextual text
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace design should favor narrative context, prose explanations, and written guidance over bare numbers and chart-heavy layouts

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces respond better to personality-driven visual elements (emojis, icons with character) than sterile, minimalist iconography, even in professional tools
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — playful vs serious
facade.label: Playful emojis
facade.acceptImplies: Incorporate expressive, character-driven visual language throughout the workspace to make interactions feel lighter and more human
facade.rejectImplies: Stick to minimal, neutral iconography and visual restraint to maintain professional credibility and reduce cognitive noise

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
